taskCenter.title: Activity
taskCenter.emptyHint: Ingestions, dataset generations, trainings, evaluations and model downloads will show up here.
taskCenter.pollingFallback: Real-time stream unavailable — falling back to polling

wizard.ingestionTodo: Upload documents to build the knowledge base

wizard.trainingTodo: Fine-tune a model on your dataset

health.apiDesc: The backend is not responding — no feature will work until it is up.
health.chatDesc: Answers, dataset generation and evaluation need it. Download a model, then restart the stack.
health.chatModelTitle: LLM server is up but no model is loaded
health.chatModelDesc: Place a .gguf file in data/models/ or download the default model.
health.embedDesc: Document ingestion and semantic search need it.
health.chromaDesc: Vector storage is unavailable — ingestion and retrieval will fail.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.chatModelHint: Run ./setup.sh --download-chat (Windows: setup.bat) or place your .gguf in data/models/
dashboard.embeddingModelHint: Run ./setup.sh --download-embed (Windows: setup.bat) or place embed.gguf in data/models/
dashboard.step1Desc: PDF, DOCX, TXT or URLs — drop your files into the Ingestion page or run adddoc.bat examples to test.
dashboard.step2Desc: Spectra automatically generates Q/A pairs from your documents. Expect 30–120 s/chunk on CPU.
dashboard.step3Desc: Pick a recipe (e.g. "Fast CPU") and fine-tune the model on your domain.
dashboard.tipPrefix: New here? Drop the files from the
dashboard.tipSuffix: to validate the pipeline in 5 minutes.
dashboard.pipelineReady: Pipeline ready — {{chunks}} chunks · {{pairs}} pairs · model ready to fine-tune
dashboard.pipelineEmpty: Empty knowledge base — ingest documents to get started (step 1)

playground.expertModeHint: Show technical details: RAG strategy badges, vector distances and latency metrics.

confirm.deleteDocMessage: “{{name}}” and its {{chunks}} indexed chunks will be permanently removed from the knowledge base. This cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteBulkMessage: The selected documents and their indexed chunks will be permanently removed from the knowledge base. This cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteModelMessage: “{{name}}” will be removed from the registry and its GGUF file ({{size}}) deleted from disk, unless another model still references it.
confirm.deleteOrphanMessage: “{{file}}” ({{size}}) is not referenced by any registered model. It will be permanently deleted from disk. This cannot be undone.
confirm.generateWhileIngestingMessage: An ingestion is still running. Starting the generation now may produce an incomplete dataset.

startup.title: Preparing Spectra
startup.description: Downloading the AI models required for the first launch…
startup.eta: This can take several minutes depending on your connection.
startup.modelsReadyDesc: Starting the inference engine… This can take a few seconds.

modelHub.subtitle: Discover the LLM models best suited to your current or simulated hardware configuration. Powered by <1>llmfit</1>.
modelHub.postInstallBody: The GGUF file was copied to <1>data/models/</1> and saved to the model registry. Once <3>activated</3> (Auto-activation here, or from the Playground), <5>llm-chat</5> reloads it automatically within a few seconds — no manual restart needed.
modelHub.loadErrorHint: Check the simulation values (e.g. "12G") and that llmfit is available on the server.
modelHub.emptyList: No model matches your filters — or llmfit returned no recommendation (check <1>docker compose logs spectra-api</1> if the list stays empty without filters).
modelHub.downloadedActive: Activated — llm-chat reloads it automatically within a few seconds.
modelHub.downloadedRegistered: Saved to the registry. Activate it from the Playground — llm-chat will reload it automatically.
modelHub.progressLostDesc: The download failed, was cancelled or interrupted (API restart). Check the logs (docker compose logs spectra-api) and relaunch the install if needed — an already downloaded file is reused.

storage.title: Storage — data/models/
storage.empty: No GGUF file in the models volume.
storage.deleteHintActive: Active model — activate another one first
storage.deleteHint: Remove from registry and delete the GGUF
storage.purged_one: {{count}} duplicate removed from the llmfit cache — {{size}} freed
storage.purged_other: {{count}} duplicates removed from the llmfit cache — {{size}} freed
storage.purgeFailed: Failed to purge the llmfit cache
storage.deleteOrphanHint: Delete this orphan file from disk

installs.title: Installation history
installs.retryHint: Relaunch this download with the same parameters

optimization.title: Response optimization
optimization.intro: Measure and <1>validate the real contribution</1> of each learning option (fine-tuning) and optimization option (RAG modules). Each <3>arm</3> changes only one thing at a time: the <5>delta</5> between two arms is the marginal gain of that option — always to be read against its latency cost.
optimization.modulesFootnote: On top comes the <1>learning</1> axis: base model vs fine-tuned model (SFT/DPO), compared via the dedicated preset.

hybrid.name: Hybrid search
hybrid.what: Combines keywords (BM25) and semantic similarity (vectors) via RRF fusion.
hybrid.gain: Better recall, robust to exact terms (references, codes).

rerank.name: Re-ranking
rerank.what: Reorders candidates with a Cross-Encoder that judges relevance precisely.
rerank.gain: More precise context on top → accuracy, Hit@k, MRR.

multiQuery.name: Multi-Query
multiQuery.what: Generates N rephrasings of the question, retrieves for each, then merges.
multiQuery.gain: Recall on ambiguous or poorly phrased questions.

corrective.name: Corrective RAG
corrective.what: Grades every retrieved chunk (LLM grading) and discards irrelevant ones.
corrective.gain: Less noise in the context → less hallucination.

compression.name: Context compression
compression.what: Extracts only the useful passages of each chunk before generation.

selfRag.name: Self-RAG
selfRag.what: The model generates then self-evaluates its answer and refines it if needed.
selfRag.gain: Faithfulness to the context, phrasing quality.

adaptive.name: Adaptive RAG
adaptive.what: Classifies the query (direct / standard / agentic) and adapts the pipeline.
adaptive.gain: Efficiency: no useless retrieval on simple questions.

conversational.name: Conversational RAG
conversational.what: Rephrases the question into a standalone one using the history.

avgScore.label: Accuracy /10
avgScore.help: Average accuracy score given by the LLM judge on answerable questions.

halluc.label: Hallucination
halluc.help: Share of questions with NO answer in the corpus where the model still made one up (lower = better).

refusal.label: Fair abstention
refusal.help: Share of unanswerable questions correctly refused ("I don't know").

hit.label: Hit@k
hit.help: Retrieval: share of questions whose expected source appears in the top-k (requires expectedSources in the benchmark).

mrr.label: MRR
mrr.help: Retrieval: average of 1/rank of the first relevant source.

recall.label: Recall@k
recall.help: Retrieval: average share of expected sources found in the top-k.

tokens.label: Context tokens
tokens.help: Deterministic cost: average number of context tokens injected into the LLM (estimated). Lower = cheaper, unlike latency (noisy).

p50.label: p50 latency
p50.help: Cost: median end-to-end latency of a query (ms). Noisy on shared hardware → cross-check with tokens.

cumulative.label: Cumulative ablation
cumulative.sub: one module added at a time

optimization.runsHint: Repetitions per arm to estimate noise (mean ± std dev). ≥3 to make deltas reliable.
optimization.slowHint: Slow on CPU: {{arms}} arms × {{runs}} run(s) × benchmark, several LLM calls per question. The run executes in the background — you can navigate away, tracking stays visible here and in the activity center.
optimization.startFailedHint: Check that the LLM and the benchmark are available.
optimization.cancelRequested: Cancellation requested — applied at the next question
optimization.cancelledBanner: Run cancelled after {{processed}} / {{total}} units. Relaunch the ablation for a complete report.
optimization.noRetrieval: Retrieval metrics (Hit@k/MRR/Recall) not computed: no benchmark question is annotated with the <1>expectedSources</1> field. Add it to measure retrieval quality.
optimization.notSignificant: Delta within noise (≤ combined σ) — not significant
optimization.multiRunNote: Values = mean over {{runs}} repetitions (±σ). A grayed-out “≈” delta is within noise (≤ combined σ): not significant.
optimization.singleRunNote: Single repetition: deltas are not tested against noise. Increase “repetitions” (≥3) to make them reliable.
optimization.appliedTitle: Modules actually triggered (validates that the option took effect)
optimization.legendDelta: Deltas in <1>green</1> indicate an improvement, in <3>red</3> a degradation, relative to the first arm.

ingestion.title: Ingestion & dataset
ingestion.dropHint: PDF, DOCX, TXT, JSON, XML, HTML, ZIP — drop files or click to select
ingestion.extractionTooltip: Method for splitting documents into semantic chunks.
ingestion.partialDesc: {{count}} file(s) could not be ingested — details in the live stream.
ingestion.oomDesc: The document is too large for the current memory configuration.
ingestion.corpusSizeTooltip: Number of chunks used to generate Q/A pairs. Start with a small sample to validate quality, then run the full corpus.
ingestion.noGeneration2: Upload documents then start the pipeline.

comparison.title: Model evaluation
comparison.abTitle: Direct head-to-head comparison between two models
comparison.batchTitle: Evaluate several models on a shared test set, then compare
comparison.compareHintDisabled: Select at least 2 completed evaluations to compare
comparison.startFailed: Failed to start a new evaluation
comparison.startFailedHint: Make sure the backend is reachable.
comparison.emptyHint: Click "New evaluation" to score the active model's quality on a dataset sample.
comparison.needTwo: Select at least 2 completed evaluations (checkboxes on the left) to compare.
comparison.compareFailed: Comparison failed. Make sure the selected evaluations are still available.
comparison.scoreDetailAria: Toggle details for score {{score}} out of 10 in category {{category}}
comparison.reportAria: Select report for model {{name}} completed at {{date}}

fineTuning.title: Fine-tuning
fineTuning.prefilledHint: fields prefilled from the active model (trainable base: {{base}})
fineTuning.baseCatalogHint: Catalog aliases: {{aliases}} — or a full HuggingFace repo ("org/name")
fineTuning.completeDesc: Adapter for {{name}} trained — export to GGUF & register to deploy
fineTuning.noActiveJob2: Click "New Training Job" to start training.
fineTuning.nameMin: Name must be at least 3 characters

documents.title: Documents
documents.subtitle: Electronic Document Management: track the lifecycle, audit changes, and manage the quality of the sources feeding your models.
documents.noMatch: No document matches the active filters.
documents.noComments: No comments. Add one manually or generate one with AI.
documents.generateHint: Describe the angle of analysis. The LLM will use RAG to retrieve the relevant passages and generate a comment grounded in the document.
documents.dpoExportHint: Approved comments (👍) can be exported as DPO pairs to fine-tune the model with your preferences.

batchEval.title: Batch evaluation
batchEval.description: Selected models are evaluated one after another on the <1>same test set</1>, then auto-selected for comparison.
batchEval.startedDesc: Models are evaluated sequentially on a shared test set. Track progress in the history.

ab.startedDesc: Generation runs per model, then a judge picks the winner per pair.
ab.empty: No A/B comparisons yet. Pick two models above and run one.

qualityBench.title: Measure quality on your corpus
qualityBench.body: The Model Hub score rates <1>hardware fit</1>. To choose on real numbers, run the held-out quality benchmark of the newly activated model (<3>{{candidate}}</3>) against the one it replaced (<5>{{baseline}}</5>).
qualityBench.alreadyRunning: A quality benchmark is already running
qualityBench.startFailed: Failed to start the quality benchmark
qualityBench.verdictBetter: ✓ <1>{{candidate}}</1> is more accurate on your corpus (+{{delta}}/10){{halluc}}.
qualityBench.verdictWorse: ✗ <1>{{candidate}}</1> scores lower on accuracy ({{delta}}/10) — the previous model may be a safer choice.
qualityBench.verdictSimilar: ≈ Both models score similarly on accuracy; decide on hallucination rate, speed and hardware fit.
qualityBench.neutralJudge: Scored by neutral judge <1>{{judge}}</1> — scores are directly comparable.
qualityBench.selfJudge: Each model scored its own answers (self-judged) — set <1>SPECTRA_EVALUATION_JUDGE_MODEL</1> for a fairer comparison.
